title: Wraparound Facilitation/Community Support - 211info

WRAPAROUND FACILITATION WITH PEER SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides wraparound services. Service includes a long-term, intensive planning process that follows a series of steps to help youth and their families reach their individual goals. Wraparound facilitators lead monthly team meetings and provide information on community resources. Peer support is offered to both youth and caregivers within the wraparound process. 1144 Gateway Loop Suite 200 Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Ages 0-20 with behavioral challenges or emotional needs Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-4:30pm Email (541) 686-5060 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KLAMATH BASIN BEHAVIORAL HEALTH KLAMATH BASIN BEHAVIORAL HEALTH ELDORADO OUTPATIENT SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Child, Adolescent, and Family Outpatient Services work with children presenting with problems of behavior, anxiety, depression, psychotic thinking, and trauma-related issues. Services include individual, family, and group therapy that focuses on strategic and structural family issues and support, trauma-related care, and wraparound services (for high-need children). 2210 Eldorado Avenue Klamath Falls, OR 97601 Eligibility: Age 18 and younger Hours: Monday-Friday 7:30am-5pm Email (541) 883-1030 Get Directions Visit Website More Details NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER EDUCATIONAL YOUTH ADVOCACY AND ENGAGEMENT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides an advocacy program that helps students and their families set and achieve goals in four key areas: Mind, Body, Spirit, and Context. Services include wrap-around services, advocacy services, college visits, life skills workshops, job training, financial aid assistance, school tutoring, after-school homework help, and the development of cultural pride and relevant skills. Incentives like stipends, meals, clothing, hygiene items, school supplies, and financial assistance for youth participants. 5135 NE Columbia Boulevard Portland, OR 97218 Eligibility: -Portland Public School District: K-12th grade self-identified Native American youth -Successful Families 2020: K-12th grade self-identified Native American youth enrolled in select schools within David Douglas and Reynolds school districts. -SUN Youth Advocacy: 5th-10th grade self-identified Native American youth enrolled in select schools within Centennial, David Douglas, Gresham-Barlow, Parkrose, Portland Public, and Reynolds school districts. OR Region 3: 5th-10th grade any race/ethnicity enrolled in the following schools: Alice Ott Middle, Cherry Park Elementary, Gilbert Heights Elementary, Gilbert Park Elementary, Parkrose High, Parkrose Middle, Prescott Elementary, Sacramento Elementary, and Shaver Elementary Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-6pm, some evening hours for events Email (503) 288-8177 329 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC CREEKSIDE CENTER CHILD & FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides individual, group, and family counseling; school-based counseling; social and behavioral skills training, wraparound services, and psychiatric and psychological evaluations. 1181 SW Ramsey Avenue Grants Pass, OR 97527 Eligibility: Parents and children experiencing mental health issues Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-6:15pm, Friday 8am-4pm (541) 476-2373 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC ILLINOIS VALLEY CENTER CHILD & FAMILY SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides individual, group, and family counseling; school-based counseling; social and behavioral skills training, wraparound services, and psychiatric and psychological evaluations. 319 Caves Highway Cave Junction, OR 97523 Eligibility: Parents and children experiencing mental health issues Hours: Monday-Thursday 8am-7pm, Friday 8am-5pm (541) 476-2373 Get Directions Visit Website More Details OUTBACK STRONG CHILDREN AND FAMILY SERVICE CHILD AND ADOLESCENT MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Child Outpatient services work with children presenting with problems of behavior, anxiety, depression, psychotic thinking, and trauma-related issues. Services include individual, family, and group therapy that focuses on strategic and structural family issues and support, trauma-related care, and wraparound services (for high need children). 35 S G Street Lakeview, OR 97630 Eligibility: Age 18 and younger Hours: Monday-Friday 8:30am-5pm (541) 947-6021 3 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD EUGENE MAIN OFFICE HUMAN SERVICES Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Case managers provide comprehensive, wraparound human and social services. Staff intervene in crisis situations with direct assistance, classes, referrals, and advocacy. Offer assistance to women and children in distress, and a safe place for runaway teenagers. 195 W 12th Avenue Eugene, OR 97401 Eligibility: Unrestricted; primarily focus is Latino and immigrant population Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-noon and 1pm-6pm Email (541) 687-2667 Get Directions Visit Website More Details PROVOKING HOPE RECOVERY SUPPORT SERVICES FACILITATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides wraparound support to parents with substance use issues. Helps support family reunification. 213 NE 10th Street McMinnville, OR 97128 Eligibility: Parents of minor children who self identify as having substance use disorder Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email (971) 228-2020 Get Directions Visit Website More Details RIVERVIEW CENTER FOR GROWTH WELLNESS AND COMMUNITY HEALTH Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Helps families experiencing barriers in their health care. Community health workers and certified application counselors provide health insurance application assistance, care coordination, wraparound support, and referrals to community resources. 3995 Marcola Road Springfield, OR 97477 Eligibility: Families with children under age 17 Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-6pm Email (541) 726-1465 Get Directions Visit Website More Details WASHINGTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH HILLSBORO RECOVERY CENTER WRAPAROUND CARE COORDINATION Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides care coordination and wraparound treatment planning for youth. Services may include community-based treatment, day treatment, treatment foster care, respite, psychiatric residential treatment, or other individualized services. 5250 NE Elam Young Parkway Hillsboro, OR 97124 Eligibility: Youth ages 0-21 must meet the following criteria: -Currently struggling in several areas such as home, school, or social relationships -Involved with two or more systems such as Special Education, Child Welfare, Juvenile Services, Mental Health, Developmental Disabilities Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 846-2288 Get Directions Visit Website More Details BENTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH CHILDREN’S OUTPATIENT PROGRAM BESTCARE TREATMENT SERVICES WRAPAROUND SERVICES BESTCARE TREATMENT SERVICES WRAPAROUND SERVICES DESCHUTES COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH CHILD AND ADOLESCENT BEHAVIORAL HEALTH SERVICES DESCHUTES COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH CHILD AND ADOLESCENT BEHAVIORAL HEALTH SERVICES DESCHUTES COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH CHILD AND ADOLESCENT BEHAVIORAL HEALTH SERVICES DIRECTION SERVICE WRAPAROUND FACILITATION WITH PEER SUPPORT KLAMATH BASIN BEHAVIORAL HEALTH OUTPATIENT SERVICES NATIVE AMERICAN YOUTH AND FAMILY CENTER EDUCATIONAL YOUTH ADVOCACY AND ENGAGEMENT OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC CHILD & FAMILY SERVICES OPTIONS FOR SOUTHERN OREGON INC CHILD & FAMILY SERVICES OUTBACK STRONG CHILD AND ADOLESCENT MENTAL HEALTH SERVICES PLAZA DE NUESTRA COMUNIDAD HUMAN SERVICES PROVOKING HOPE RECOVERY SUPPORT SERVICES FACILITATION RIVERVIEW CENTER FOR GROWTH WELLNESS AND COMMUNITY HEALTH WASHINGTON COUNTY BEHAVIORAL HEALTH WRAPAROUND CARE COORDINATION
